pkg/framework/transport/httptransport.purpose: Generic typed HTTP handler pipeline (decode → operation → encode) shared by all domain httpdriver and api/v3/handlers packages. Handler[Request,Response] is the universal adapter between Chi routes and domain service calls; every v1 and v3 endpoint is built on it.

patterns.name: NewHandler triple constructor
patterns.description: Always construct via NewHandler(requestDecoder, op, responseEncoder, ...options). Never instantiate handler{} directly — defaultHandlerOptions (GenericErrorEncoder) are injected by newHandler and would be missing.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs for path-param injection
patterns.description: When a Chi URL param must be baked into the decoder, use NewHandlerWithArgs + .With(arg) at mount time. With() clones the value-receiver handler, binding the arg into decodeRequest without allocation on each request.
patterns.example: h := httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decodeGetFoo, op, encodeFoo); r.Get("/{id}", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { h.With(chi.URLParam(r, "id")).ServeHTTP(w, r) })

patterns.name: ErrorEncoder chain (first match wins)
patterns.description: WithErrorEncoder appends to h.errorEncoders; handlers iterate in order; the first returning true short-circuits. GenericErrorEncoder is appended last by defaultHandlerOptions — custom domain encoders must be passed as options to NewHandler, not after Chain.

patterns.name: Chain for cross-cutting middleware
patterns.description: Use handler.Chain(outer, ...others) to wrap the operation with operation.Middleware. Chain returns a new handler value (value-receiver copy); the original is unmodified.

patterns.name: SelfEncodingError escape hatch
patterns.description: Errors implementing SelfEncodingError (EncodeError(ctx, w) bool) bypass the ErrorEncoder chain. Use only for infrastructure error types (e.g. models.StatusProblem). Do not implement on domain errors — use ErrorEncoder instead.
patterns.example: // models.StatusProblem implements SelfEncodingError and calls Respond(w) directly

key_file_guides.role: Core Handler[Req,Resp] interface and internal handler struct. ServeHTTP orchestrates decode→operation→encode; encodeError iterates the chain then falls back to SelfEncodingError then 500. defaultHandlerOptions appends GenericErrorEncoder unconditionally.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not pass GenericErrorEncoder again via WithErrorEncoder — it is already appended by defaultHandlerOptions; a second copy double-encodes errors.

key_file_guides.role: HandlerWithArgs variant for route-param injection. With() clones the value-receiver handler and swaps decodeRequest; relies on handler being a non-pointer struct.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Both With() and Chain() rely on value-receiver copy semantics. If pointer fields are ever added to handler, these methods will share state across copies — explicit deep copy required.

key_file_guides.role: HandlerOption interface and all WithX constructors. errorEncoders slice is order-sensitive (append). resolveErrorHandler returns dummyErrorHandler when none set.
key_file_guides.watch_for: errorEncoders are appended in order; custom domain encoders passed after defaultHandlerOptions are appended after GenericErrorEncoder and will never fire — always pass custom encoders as options to NewHandler.

key_file_guides.role: Declares ResponseEncoder[T] and ErrorEncoder pure function types. No logic — pure type definitions that both handler.go and commonhttp import without cycle.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ErrorEncoder must return false if it did not write to ResponseWriter; returning true after a partial write leaves the response in an inconsistent state.

anti_patterns: Instantiating handler{} struct directly — bypasses defaultHandlerOptions and loses GenericErrorEncoder
Calling context.Background() inside a RequestDecoder — always use the ctx passed from ServeHTTP
Implementing SelfEncodingError on domain-level errors — reserve it for infrastructure types; domain errors belong in ErrorEncoder chain
Adding state (mutexes, caches) to HandlerWithArgs or handler — value types cloned by With() and Chain() would race on shared pointer fields
Writing to ResponseWriter inside RequestDecoder on error — return the error and let the ErrorEncoder chain handle it

decisions.rationale: Enables safe per-request arg injection and middleware wrapping without allocation or locking; the original registered handler is immutable after construction.

decisions.decision: ErrorEncoder chain with last-resort fallback to 500 in handler.go, not in GenericErrorEncoder
decisions.rationale: Keeps the fallback authoritative and unconfigurable; any error not handled by the chain or SelfEncodingError always produces a well-formed 500 problem+json response.

decisions.decision: Separate encoder sub-package for ResponseEncoder/ErrorEncoder function types
decisions.rationale: Breaks the import cycle between handler.go (which uses both) and commonhttp (which defines GenericErrorEncoder) — encoder has no dependencies so both can import it.

code_examples.scenario: Minimal domain HTTP handler wired into a Chi router
